title: (No AI) Anthropic’s Claude Tag is learning your company, one Slack message at a time
dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan

title: (No AI) Google Home will soon get better at recognizing you
author: Stevie Bonifield
content:
![A Google Home logo on a blue and white illustration with the Matter logo.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/23451196/acastro_220510_STK123_0002.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

A new [update](https://support.google.com/googlehome/answer/15962877?hl=en#zippy=%2Cjune) for Google Home could make it less likely your smart home cameras mistake you for someone else, just because you're facing away from the camera. Starting June 23rd, Google's expanding its facial recognition feature so that people you've tagged in your Familiar Faces library can continue to be identified when their faces aren't clearly visible, using "additional non-biometric signals (body size, clothing color, etc.)."

The Familiar Faces library will also begin automatically updating with the most recent images of everyone in your house, so you should get fewer inaccurate notifications from outdated examples.

Google also says …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/tech/955385/google-home-familiar-faces-clothing)
contentSnippet:
A new update for Google Home could make it less likely your smart home cameras mistake you for someone else, just because you're facing away from the camera. Starting June 23rd, Google's expanding its facial recognition feature so that people you've tagged in your Familiar Faces library can continue to be identified when their faces aren't clearly visible, using "additional non-biometric signals (body size, clothing color, etc.)." 
The Familiar Faces library will also begin automatically updating with the most recent images of everyone in your house, so you should get fewer inaccurate notifications from outdated examples.
Google also says  …
Read the full story at The Verge.

Starting June 23rd, Google's expanding its facial recognition feature so that people you've tagged in your Familiar Faces library can continue to be identified when their faces […]

title: (No AI) Hollywood is bending the knee to OpenAI
author: Charles Pulliam-Moore
content:
![Photo collage of Sam Altman in front of the OpenAI logo.](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/chorus/uploads/chorus_asset/file/25431704/STK201_SAM_ALTMAN_CVIRGINIA_C.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=4.7487503051758,18.543864880511,95.251249694824,81.456135119489)

Netflix, A24, Focus Features, and Warner Bros.' Clockwork have all [reportedly](https://variety.com/2026/film/global/luca-guadagnino-artificial-sam-altman-netflix-a24-mubi-1236786623/) decided to pass on picking up [_Artificial_](https://www.theverge.com/news/679007/theres-going-to-be-a-movie-about-openai-ceo-sam-altmans-ouster-and-subsequent-rehire) \- director Luca Guadagnino's new biographical drama about OpenAI cofounder / CEO Sam Altman - for distribution deals. And while [Neon and Mubi](https://www.vulture.com/article/luca-guadagninos-sam-altman-movie-mubi-neon.html) are still said to be interested in the film, this situation makes it seem like Hollywood no longer has the courage to tell critical stories about Big Tech.

Postproduction on [_Artificial_](https://www.theverge.com/news/679007/theres-going-to-be-a-movie-about-openai-ceo-sam-altmans-ouster-and-subsequent-rehire) was nearly finished when [Amazon MGM unexpectedly announced last week](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/952906/sam-altman-film-artificial-openai-amazon-mgm-dropped) that it no longer plans to distribute the film. The news came as a surprise given how far along the movie was and reports that [Amazon …](https://www.forbes.com/sites/maryroeloffs/2026/06/22/what-we-know-about-the-future-of-amazons-dropped-sam-altman-film-biopic-after-amazon-netflix-passed/)

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/entertainment/954899/luca-guadagnino-artificial-sam-altman-amazon-a24-neon-mubi-chatgpt)
contentSnippet:
Netflix, A24, Focus Features, and Warner Bros.' Clockwork have all reportedly decided to pass on picking up Artificial - director Luca Guadagnino's new biographical drama about OpenAI cofounder / CEO Sam Altman - for distribution deals. And while Neon and Mubi are still said to be interested in the film, this situation makes it seem like Hollywood no longer has the courage to tell critical stories about Big Tech.
Postproduction on Artificial was nearly finished when Amazon MGM unexpectedly announced last week that it no longer plans to distribute the film. The news came as a surprise given how far along the movie was and reports that Amazon …
Read the full story at The Verge.
summary: Netflix, A24, Focus Features, and Warner Bros.' Clockwork have all reportedly decided to pass on picking up Artificial - director Luca Guadagnino's new biographical drama about OpenAI cofounder / CEO Sam Altman - for distribution deals. And while Neon and Mubi are still said to be interested in the film, this situation makes it seem […]
